name: Nick Westbrook-Ikhine
headline: Field-stretching Colts WR with TD-dependent boom-bust profile
body:
Westbrook-Ikhine signed with Indianapolis after a brutal 2025 in Miami, where his role cratered: 13 games, a 44.2 percent snap share, just 20 targets and a 4.3 percent target share for 89 yards and zero touchdowns on a thin 0.42 yards per route. That collapse followed his career year in Tennessee in 2024, when he turned a 69.9 percent snap rate and 60 targets into 497 yards and a remarkable nine touchdowns, posting 103.7 fantasy points and a 71 positional rank.

The gap between those seasons exposes the profile: a deep-ball, low-volume specialist whose value lives almost entirely on touchdowns. His 21.88 air yards per reception in 2024 shows the big-play role, but 1.20 yards per route that year against 0.42 in 2025 shows how usage-dependent he is. In a crowded Colts receiver room he projects as a rotational field-stretcher, not a target earner. He is a waiver-wire name and a touchdown-chasing bye-week dart at best, not a startable weekly option.
